answer.text: The market's expectation of future volatility over the life of the option
The historical volatility of the underlying asset
The actual volatility that will occur in the future
The volatility set by SEBI for margin calculation

explanation.text: **The Logic:** IV is derived from the market price of an option by reverse-engineering the Black-Scholes formula. It represents consensus among market participants about expected future price fluctuation. **The Trap:** IV ≠ historical volatility (past) or realized volatility (actual future). It's the MARKET'S EXPECTATION. **Cross-Exam Context:** IV is tested in Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** IV generally increases in bearish markets (more fear/uncertainty) and decreases in bullish markets.
